en: So there's light calvary and heavy calvary,
ko: 경기병과 중기병이 있고

en: and breaking down units using the decimal system, 10, 100, 1,000.
ko: 10진법으로 부대를 나눠요, 10, 100, 1,000 단위로.

en: So there's a kind of hierarchy
ko: 일종의 위계가 있어서

en: where you delegate authority,
ko: 권한을 위임하지만

en: but to the degree there's commands, they must be followed strictly.
ko: 명령이 내려지면 엄격히 따라야 해요.

en: Yes. So for like extremely efficient, accurate,
ko: 네. 매우 효율적이고 정확한

en: precise deployment of these troops in the battlefield
ko: 전장에서의 정밀한 병력 배치와

en: and the dynamic movement of the troops, including all the interesting tactics that were utilized,
ko: 흥미로운 전술을 포함한 역동적인 병력 이동을 위해선

en: you have to have really good communication and coordination.
ko: 정말 좋은 소통과 조율이 필요해요.

en: And for that, orders must be followed. Is there something to speak to that?
ko: 그래서 명령을 따라야 하죠. 그것에 대해 말씀해주실 게 있나요?

en: Like how do you tune this kind of system to where everybody is working together so well?
ko: 어떻게 이런 체계를 조율해서 모두가 그렇게 잘 협력하게 만드나요?

en: I think the first point is the extreme loyalty of the people whom Chinggis Khan chose.
ko: 첫 번째는 칭기스 칸이 선택한 사람들의 극한 충성심이라고 생각해요.

en: His kinsmen, as we said, had deserted him.
ko: 앞서 말했듯이 그의 친족들은 그를 버렸죠.

en: His anda was a Christian relationship,
ko: 그의 안다는 기독교적 관계였고

en: but all the others that he found were just common people, herders or hunters, very common,
ko: 나머지는 그냥 평범한 사람들, 목축민이나 사냥꾼이었어요.

en: and they were loyal to him and never, ever revolted against him, never betrayed him.
ko: 그들은 충성스러웠고 절대 반란하거나 배신하지 않았어요.

en: So he had extreme loyalty. And then, as you mentioned, he organized his decimal system
ko: 극한 충성심이 있었죠. 그리고 말씀하신 대로 10진법 체계를 조직했는데

en: so the smallest unit of the army was the arft,
ko: 가장 작은 부대 단위는 아르프트로

en: the squad of 10 men.
ko: 10명의 분대였어요.

en: They were put together and then the head of that squad, he had total control over it.
ko: 함께 편성되고 분대장이 완전한 통제권을 가졌죠.

en: But the men knew that they were going to protect each other
ko: 하지만 대원들은 서로를 보호할 거라는 걸 알았고

en: and they had to come back with every member or everybody.
ko: 모든 대원과 함께 돌아와야 했어요.

en: You don't leave anybody behind.
ko: 아무도 뒤에 남기지 않아요.

en: So this was extremely important. So if you submit to the orders of the man in charge,
ko: 이게 매우 중요했죠. 지휘관의 명령에 복종하면

en: you know that he's risking his own life for you also.
ko: 그도 당신을 위해 목숨을 걸고 있다는 걸 알아요.

en: And you know that your brother on the left and on the right is risking his life for you.
ko: 좌우의 형제가 당신을 위해 목숨을 걸고 있다는 것도요.

en: The army was, they were organized with five horses, each man.
ko: 군대는 1인당 말 5마리로 편성됐어요.

en: They had their bow and they had a lot of arrows, as many as they could have,
ko: 활과 가능한 한 많은 화살을 가지고 있었고

en: but they also retrieved arrows at the end of their battle.
ko: 전투 후에 화살도 회수했어요.

en: And they also would retrieve the enemy arrows.
ko: 적의 화살도 회수했고요.

en: This was a great advantage, by the way, when they hit Russia
ko: 참고로 러시아를 공격할 때 큰 이점이었는데

en: because the Russians could not use Mongolian arrows.
ko: 러시아인들은 몽골 화살을 쓸 수 없었거든요.

en: They could knock them in their bow, but the Mongols could use Russian arrows.
ko: 활에 끼울 수는 있었지만, 몽골인들은 러시아 화살을 쓸 수 있었어요.

en: So all these little things, but it's not even just the arrow.
ko: 이런 사소한 것들이 다인데, 화살만이 아니에요.

en: Also, they had to carry needle and thread.
ko: 바늘과 실도 가지고 다녀야 했어요.

en: Every soldier had to be able to sew.
ko: 모든 병사가 바느질을 할 줄 알아야 했죠.

en: And sometimes that could be a torn garment.
ko: 때로는 찢어진 옷이 될 수도 있고

en: It could be a piece of skin or a wound that somebody has.
ko: 피부 조각이나 누군가의 상처가 될 수도 있었어요.

en: It was a very odd thing when you think about the army of Chinggis Khan and they're carrying everything themselves.
ko: 칭기스 칸의 군대가 모든 걸 직접 들고 다녔다니 이상하죠.

en: They don't have any pack train behind them. And that one of the things they have to carry is needle and thread
ko: 뒤에 보급대가 없었거든요. 그런데 가져가야 할 것 중에 바늘과 실이 있었어요.

en: in order to sew up things. So complete self-reliance in that regard.
ko: 꿰매기 위해서요. 그 면에서 완전한 자급자족이었죠.

en: Yes. They also carried dry dairy products,
ko: 네. 건조 유제품도 가지고 다녔어요.

en: it's called, where they dry curd and they can keep it for a couple of years even.
ko: 응유를 건조시킨 건데 몇 년까지도 보관할 수 있었어요.

en: But you dry it and then when you need it, you can put it in a flask of water.
ko: 건조시켜놓고 필요할 때 물통에 넣으면 돼요.

en: You ride all day. It joggles up and down, boom, boom, boom, and turns into kind of thick protein.
ko: 하루 종일 말을 타면 흔들려서 걸쭉한 단백질이 돼요.

en: It's said that the Mongols could easily go three to five days without ever building a fire.
ko: 몽골인들은 불을 피우지 않고 3~5일을 쉽게 갈 수 있었다고 해요.

en: They had enough food. So all these little things at the lowest level were important
ko: 충분한 식량이 있었죠. 이런 최하위 수준의 사소한 것들이 중요했어요.

en: as well at the highest level of his loyalty, of his men to him.
ko: 최고 수준의 충성심만큼이나요.

en: And it went all the way down. Loyalty was extremely important.
ko: 위에서 아래까지 이어졌어요. 충성심이 극히 중요했죠.

en: And he organized the army into left wing, right wing, or east and west.
ko: 그는 군대를 좌익, 우익 또는 동서로 편성했어요.

en: Mongols, the word for left is east, the word for right is west.
ko: 몽골어로 왼쪽은 동쪽, 오른쪽은 서쪽이에요.

en: So those two wings. And then in the middle was the gol, the center,
ko: 그 두 날개가 있고 가운데는 골, 중심이었는데

en: this moving center that was his bodyguard
ko: 이동하는 중심으로 그의 친위대이자

en: and his unit in the middle. Then usually they'd have a vanguard and a rear guard.
ko: 그의 부대가 가운데 있었어요. 보통 선봉대와 후위대도 있었고요.

en: Sometimes the vanguard would go out as much as two years in advance to clear the land,
ko: 선봉대가 최대 2년 전에 미리 나가서 땅을 정리하기도 했어요.

en: run the people away, scare them, make them go away so that the grass is left there for the army
ko: 사람들을 쫓아내고 겁줘서 떠나게 해서 군대를 위해 풀이 남아있게 하죠.

en: when it moves through. And they never marched the way other armies do in a line
ko: 군대가 지나갈 때요. 다른 군대처럼 한 줄로 행군한 적도 없었어요.

en: of one following the other. They would always go in long lines spread out in wings
ko: 하나가 다른 하나를 따라가는 식이 아니라 날개처럼 펼쳐진 긴 줄로 갔어요.

en: so that each horse is on its own path, you could say,
ko: 각 말이 자기만의 길에 있도록요.

en: but all parallel together. So they had very precise ways of doing things.
ko: 하지만 모두 평행하게요. 매우 정밀한 방식이 있었어요.

en: And this, I think, was the secret with him.
ko: 이게 그의 비결이었다고 생각해요.

en: And he used the best people,
ko: 최고의 사람들을 썼고

en: but he also, he was willing to train them as much as possible.
ko: 가능한 한 많이 훈련시킬 의지도 있었어요.

en: He never punished them for what happened.
ko: 일어난 일로 벌하지 않았어요.

en: So Shiki Hutuk, for example, the supreme judge, he was in command one time of a group in a battle
ko: 예를 들어 대법관 시키 후투크가 한번 전투에서 부대를 지휘했는데

en: in Afghanistan, and he lost the battle, which is very, very unusual for Mongols.
ko: 아프가니스탄에서 전투를 졌어요. 몽골에겐 매우 드문 일이죠.

en: So Genghis Khan went out with him, said, okay, let's go to the battlefield together
ko: 그래서 칭기스 칸이 함께 나가서 말했어요, 좋아 같이 전장에 가자.

en: and look it over. And you explain to me what you did, and then we will talk about it.
ko: 살펴보고 네가 뭘 했는지 설명해줘, 그리고 얘기하자.

en: So he was very thoughtful in the way that he was training the people around him.
ko: 주변 사람들을 훈련시키는 방식이 매우 사려 깊었어요.

en: And they knew they weren't gonna be punished. It's not like these countries where the general comes back
ko: 벌받지 않을 거란 걸 알았어요. 장군이 돌아오면 처형당하는 나라들과 달랐죠.

en: and gets executed because he lost. No, Genghis Khan knows every general is gonna try 100%.
ko: 칭기스 칸은 모든 장군이 100% 노력할 거란 걸 알았어요.

en: And if they retreat, fine, they're saving Mongol lives.
ko: 후퇴해도 괜찮아요, 몽골인의 생명을 살리는 거니까.

en: They know what to do. He respects that.
ko: 그들은 뭘 해야 하는지 알아요. 그는 그걸 존중했죠.

en: So all these things like that fit together.
ko: 이런 것들이 다 맞물렸어요.

en: But I think a part of it that was important for him,
ko: 하지만 그에게 중요했던 부분 중 하나는

en: so he had this base from steppe warfare already,
ko: 초원 전쟁의 기반이 이미 있었고

en: the horse, the archery, and how that all fit together.
ko: 말, 궁술, 그리고 그 모든 것이 어우러지는 방식이요.

en: But he was very quick to embrace any kind
ko: 하지만 어떤 종류의

en: of other technology that he saw.
ko: 다른 기술이든 빠르게 수용했어요.

en: I think that sedentary armies, like sedentary civilizations,
ko: 정착 군대는 정착 문명처럼

en: they get stuck in their ways. This is how we do it.
ko: 자기 방식에 갇히죠. 우리는 이렇게 해.

en: And we're gonna make it a little faster. We're gonna make it a little bigger, a little stronger.
ko: 좀 더 빠르게, 좀 더 크게, 좀 더 강하게 하겠지만

en: But this is how we think. Genghis Khan had no set way to think.
ko: 사고방식은 이거야. 칭기스 칸은 정해진 사고방식이 없었어요.

en: And when he encountered the first walled cities
ko: 처음 성벽 도시를 만났을 때

en: around 1209, after founding his nation in 1206, he went out on these raids.
ko: 1206년 나라를 세운 후 1209년경 원정을 나갔어요.

en: And I really think they were raids, not wars at first.
ko: 처음에는 전쟁이 아니라 습격이었다고 생각해요.

en: So he went into Tangut territory of what's now Northwestern China
ko: 지금의 중국 서북부인 탕구트 영토에 들어갔어요.

en: in the upper reaches of the Yellow River. So he went there.
ko: 황하 상류 지역으로요.

en: And of course, the cities have walls around them. This is a man who's never encountered a wall in his life.
ko: 도시에는 성벽이 있었죠. 평생 벽을 본 적 없는 사람이에요.

en: Well, he did, but they were made out of felt.
ko: 본 적은 있지만 펠트로 만든 거였죠.

en: The walls around his tent are, you know, felt walls.
ko: 텐트 벽이 펠트 벽이니까요.

en: Yeah, just imagine what it's like for the first time in your life seeing a wall.
ko: 네, 인생에서 처음으로 벽을 보는 게 어떤 건지 상상해보세요.

en: Yes. When you come from the Mongolian steppe, where there's no, there's very few
ko: 네. 벽 같은 자연물이 거의 없는

en: even natural wall-like things. Right.
ko: 몽골 초원에서 오면요. 맞아요.

en: Well, they have like the wall cliffs in some places.
ko: 일부 지역에 절벽 같은 건 있어요.

en: They're familiar with that, and they can climb them. But they don't have people at the top
ko: 그건 익숙하고 오를 수도 있죠. 하지만 꼭대기에서

en: shooting down the mountain, but on the, so he looked, but he looked at everything around him
ko: 쏘는 사람은 없었죠. 그래서 그는 주변 모든 것을 관찰했어요.

en: and he saw, okay, they have this river and they have all these channels and they're always moving water around.
ko: 강이 있고 수로가 있고 항상 물을 이동시키는 걸 봤어요.

en: And like we said, for a Mongol, anything that moves is a potential weapon.
ko: 앞서 말했듯이 몽골인에게 움직이는 건 뭐든 잠재적 무기예요.

en: Anything that doesn't move is a target. You've got moving water.
ko: 움직이지 않는 건 표적이고요. 움직이는 물이 있어요.

en: You've got a standing non-moving wall.
ko: 움직이지 않는 성벽이 있고요.

en: So he said, okay, the men are gonna dig a channel
ko: 그래서 말했죠, 좋아 수로를 파서

en: and they're gonna bring down the wall of the Tangut city.
ko: 탕구트 도시의 성벽을 무너뜨리겠다.

en: Well, they did it and they didn't know exactly what they were doing and the embankments weren't high enough
ko: 실행했는데 정확히 뭘 하는지 몰랐고 제방이 충분히 높지 않아서

en: and too much water came in from the Yellow River and actually flooded out the Mongol camp.
ko: 황하에서 물이 너무 많이 들어와서 오히려 몽골 진영이 침수됐어요.

en: But, okay, it happened. We learned that lesson, so we're gonna improve it.
ko: 하지만 괜찮아, 일어난 거니까. 교훈을 얻었으니 개선하겠다.

en: And that became a strategy that actually worked
ko: 그게 실제로 효과가 있는 전략이 됐어요.

en: for the Mongols for the next 50 years, all the way to Baghdad.
ko: 50년간 바그다드까지 몽골에게요.

en: They were able to use it when they conquered Baghdad in 1258.
ko: 1258년 바그다드 정복 때도 쓸 수 있었죠.

en: So this ability to see things and to try them
ko: 이렇게 사물을 관찰하고 시도하는 능력

en: and if they fail to try them in a different way, but a better way, we all think we learned from our mistakes.
ko: 실패하면 다른, 더 나은 방식으로 시도하는 거죠. 우리 모두 실수에서 배운다고 생각하잖아요.

en: We all, oh yeah, yeah, I learned from that. And what do we do?
ko: 다들 아 그래, 거기서 배웠지. 근데 뭘 하죠?

en: We repeat the mistake. I think it's just a part of human nature.
ko: 같은 실수를 반복해요. 인간 본성의 일부인 것 같아요.

en: Well, it didn't work the first eight times, but I'm gonna do it one more time.
ko: 처음 8번은 안 됐지만 한 번 더 해볼 거야.

en: I think it's gonna work. I know I'm gonna win the lottery this time because I got the right.
ko: 이번엔 될 거야. 이번엔 복권 당첨될 거야.

en: That's how we think.
ko: 우리가 그렇게 생각하죠.

en: But he had that real ability to, first of all,
ko: 하지만 그에겐 무엇보다

en: to be humble before these other things he didn't know about, technology,
ko: 자기가 모르는 기술 앞에서 겸손할 수 있는 능력이 있었어요.

en: and to understand that he didn't understand, but he could understand it in his own way.
ko: 이해 못 한다는 걸 이해하면서도 자기만의 방식으로 이해할 수 있었죠.

en: And he did. Over and over, the Mongols were excellent at putting together new things in new ways
ko: 그랬어요. 반복적으로 몽골인들은 새로운 것을 새로운 방식으로 조합하는 데 탁월했어요.

en: and using them against their enemies.
ko: 그리고 적에게 활용했죠.

en: So rapid, extreme, continued innovation.
ko: 빠르고 극단적이며 지속적인 혁신이요.

en: So you couple that with a,
ko: 그걸

en: I mean, you have to say a revolutionary idea
ko: 혁명적 아이디어와 결합하면

en: that promotion should be based on merit.
ko: 승진이 실력에 기반해야 한다는.

en: That idea combined with the innovative approach
ko: 그 아이디어가 혁신적 접근과 결합되면

en: to the military, it just feeds on itself
ko: 군사적으로 자체 강화되는 거예요.

en: because the people who are learning from their mistakes and constantly improving
ko: 실수에서 배우고 끊임없이 개선하는 사람들이

en: are the ones that get promoted in the positions of power, and then they inspire everybody else to do the same.
ko: 권력의 자리에 승진하고 다른 모두에게 영감을 주죠.

en: And so it's just, if every action is judged based on the excellence of that action,
ko: 모든 행동이 그 행동의 탁월함으로 평가된다면

en: then over time, repeated iteration in war
ko: 시간이 지나면서 전쟁에서의 반복적 개선이

en: creates a more and more powerful army.
ko: 점점 더 강력한 군대를 만들어요.

en: Yes, yes. And they were able to do that for three generations,
ko: 네, 네. 3세대에 걸쳐 그렇게 할 수 있었어요.

en: to create an army that was ever expanding,
ko: 계속 확장하고

en: ever changing its tactics and its technology.
ko: 전술과 기술을 끊임없이 바꾸는 군대를요.

en: And they got worse at it over time.
ko: 시간이 지나면서 점점 못하게 됐지만.

en: But Chinggis Khan was the one who innovated it. He was the best with it.
ko: 칭기스 칸이 혁신한 사람이었어요. 그가 최고였죠.

en: And he used it throughout his lifetime, and he was getting better over his lifetime
ko: 평생에 걸쳐 사용했고 점점 더 잘하게 됐어요.

en: with using foreign information, foreign technology, foreign ideas.
ko: 외국의 정보, 기술, 아이디어를 활용하는 데.

en: He just had a genius for that.
ko: 그에 대한 천재성이 있었어요.
